item.title: His Last Call Ended with Chilling Words
item.description: Emma Kenny discusses a final phone call and the unsettling words that followed.

media:content.media:title: His Last Call Ended with Chilling Words
media:content.media:description: Emma Kenny discusses a final phone call and the unsettling words that followed.

item.title: Emma Kenny on Rurik Jutting's case
item.description: Rurik Jutting, a former British banker, was convicted in Hong Kong of murdering two Indonesian women, Sumarti Ningsih and Seneng Mujiasih, in his apartment in 2014. Prosecutors said the case involved prolonged abuse, and the trial exposed disturbing video and photo evidence recorded by Jutting himself. In November 2016, a jury found him guilty of two counts of murder, and he was sentenced to life imprisonment. What do cases like this reveal about how easily extreme violence can hide behind an ordinary public image?

media:content.media:title: Emma Kenny on Rurik Jutting's case
media:content.media:description: Rurik Jutting, a former British banker, was convicted in Hong Kong of murdering two Indonesian women, Sumarti Ningsih and Seneng Mujiasih, in his apartment in 2014. Prosecutors said the case involved prolonged abuse, and the trial exposed disturbing video and photo evidence recorded by Jutting himself. In November 2016, a jury found him guilty of two counts of murder, and he was sentenced to life imprisonment. What do cases like this reveal about how easily extreme violence can hide behind an ordinary public image?
